explicit_text: I went to Camp Roberts in California for my basic training in the Field Artillery during World War II. It was around late 1943 and early 1944, right after I was drafted. The training was tough but interesting, and I remember some funny moments like climbing trees at night with a flashlight to sign my name. Camp Roberts was where I first got a real taste of military life away from home.
